columns.Name: EVENT_ID
columns.Comments: System-generated primary key column.

columns.Name: BUSINESS_GROUP_ID
columns.Comments: Identifier of Enterprise, used for multi-tenancy partitioning. Foreign key to HR_ORGANIZATION_UNITS.

columns.Name: LOCATION_ID
columns.Comments: Foreign key to HR_LOCATIONS.

columns.Name: INTERNAL_CONTACT_PERSON_ID
columns.Comments: Foreign key to PER_PEOPLE.

columns.Name: ORGANIZATION_RUN_BY_ID
columns.Comments: Foreign key to HR_ORGANIZATION_UNITS.

columns.Name: ASSIGNMENT_ID
columns.Comments: Foreign key to PER_ASSIGNMENTS.

columns.Name: DATE_START
columns.Comments: Start date of event or interview.

columns.Name: TYPE
columns.Comments: Event type or interview name.

columns.Name: CONTACT_TELEPHONE_NUMBER
columns.Comments: External contact telephone number.

columns.Name: DATE_END
columns.Comments: End date of event or interview.

columns.Name: EMP_OR_APL
columns.Comments: Indicates an employee or applicant (E=employee and A=applicant).

columns.Name: EVENT_OR_INTERVIEW
columns.Comments: Indicates an event or interview (E = event and I = interview.

columns.Name: EXTERNAL_CONTACT
columns.Comments: Name of the external contact.  Only used for events.

columns.Name: TIME_END
columns.Comments: Time the event or interview is expected to end.

columns.Name: TIME_START
columns.Comments: Time the event or interview is expected to start.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the last update of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_LOGIN
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the session login associated to the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: CREATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who created the row.

columns.Name: CREATION_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the creation of the row.

columns.Name: OBJECT_VERSION_NUMBER
columns.Comments: Used to implement optimistic locking. This number is incremented every time that the row is updated. The number is compared at the start and end of a transaction to detect whether another session has updated the row since it was queried.
